does: This paper fine-tunes GPT-3 to answer complex, long-form questions by giving it access to a text-based web-browsing environment where it can search, navigate, and collect references to support its responses. The authors train the model using behavior cloning from human demonstrations on the ELI5 dataset, then refine it through rejection sampling guided by a reward model trained on human preference judgments. The resulting system produces answers preferred by human evaluators 56% of the time compared to expert demonstrators and 69% of the time compared to top Reddit answers, while maintaining explicit reference citations that facilitate verification of factual claims.
connects: This work builds on the intersection of several research directions: scaling language models like GPT-3 to handle complex reasoning tasks, reinforcement learning from human feedback approaches developed for aligning large models, imitation learning methods that leverage human demonstrations, and information retrieval systems that augment language models with external knowledge. It extends prior work on using human feedback to improve model outputs and combines this with the controlled task setup of question-answering benchmarks. The emphasis on reference collection responds to concerns about factual accuracy and hallucination in large language models, connecting to broader efforts to ground model outputs in verifiable sources.
enables: This work opens paths toward more reliable and auditable AI systems by demonstrating that language models can learn to cite sources while answering questions, enabling human verification of factual claims. It suggests how reinforcement learning from human feedback can be applied to complex, multi-step reasoning tasks involving external information retrieval. Future work could extend this to other domains requiring factual grounding, explore more sophisticated browsing strategies, develop better methods for evaluating long-form answer quality, and investigate how to scale these techniques to models beyond GPT-3. The approach also raises questions about combining web search with language models in production systems and how to handle misinformation or outdated information encountered during browsing.
